steps.title: Where does the agent run? (infra fork)
Which product shape do you optimize for?

options.label: OSS SDK / runtime (e.g. Ollama, llama.cpp) + DIY
Desktop app (e.g. LM Studio, Jan, Osaurus)

steps.title: Future scenario: how do bots pay each other? (design only)

options.label: No money rail — internal compute metering only

outcomes.title: Aligned with QVAC + USDT horizon (demo)
outcomes.body: Local-first + QVAC suite + USDT between bots in a sandbox policy. Legal and issuer constraints still map to roadmap work.

outcomes.title: Local OSS SDK without a payment rail
outcomes.body: Great for privacy and prototypes; lacks the unified micropayment / agent-economy story QVAC can channel over time.

outcomes.title: Desktop app — user-facing, no payment rail
outcomes.body: Polished UX for end-users running models locally. Payment and agent coordination are not part of the default story.

outcomes.title: Cloud + OSS runtime under heavy gating
outcomes.body: Possible but high friction: cloud dependency + strict compliance narrows agent autonomy to known counterparties.

defaultOutcome.title: Hybrid mix
defaultOutcome.body: Selections suggest partly automatable flows with policy exceptions or manual edges — a typical cross-team implementation backlog.
